name: Australia
summary: English-speaking points-based study->485->skilled-PR route with full spouse work rights, but software engineers left the 2025 shortage list, ICT PR is fiercely competitive, and Bangladesh sits at Evidence Level 3 with steep fees.

job-market.summary: Solid tech demand and high salaries, but software engineers left the 2025 national shortage list.
job-market.reasoning: Jobs and Skills Australia's 2025 Occupation Shortage List removed software/developer roles from shortage in every state (AI adoption plus more local graduates), though cybersecurity and AI-specialist roles stay short and the sector still needs ~312,000 workers by 2030; sponsorship for generic SW roles is now harder for both partners.
evidence: Software engineers no longer in shortage in any state (JSA 2025)
Median SW engineer pay ~AUD 128k-151k; AI/cyber roles still in demand
Australia needs ~312,000 extra tech workers by 2030

links.title: Jobs and Skills Australia Occupation Shortage List

visa-access.summary: Bangladesh at Evidence Level 3 (highest scrutiny); no Dhaka decision office, AUD 2,000 fee, AUD 29,710 funds.
visa-access.reasoning: From January 2026 Bangladesh sits at Evidence Level 3, triggering the deepest document, financial and bank verification; applicants lodge biometrics via VFS, the student-visa charge rose to AUD 2,000 (Jul 2025), proof of AUD 29,710 living costs is required, and the Genuine Student test plus falling approval rates raise refusal risk.
evidence: Bangladesh reclassified to Evidence Level 3 (Jan 2026), highest scrutiny
Student visa charge AUD 2,000 since 1 Jul 2025; living-cost proof AUD 29,710
Genuine Student requirement; direct bank/employer verification, lump-sum deposits flagged

citizenship.summary: Citizenship after 4 years lawful residence incl. last 12 months as PR; dual citizenship allowed.
citizenship.reasoning: Applicants need 4 years lawful residence with the final 12 months as a permanent resident and limited absences, but PR must be secured first (a hard, competitive step for ICT); Australia has allowed dual citizenship since 2002 with only a modest English/citizenship test.
evidence: 4 years lawful residence incl. last 12 months as PR
Absence limits: <12 months over 4 years, <90 days in final year
Dual citizenship permitted; modest citizenship test

post-study-work.summary: Subclass 485 ~2 years for coursework masters, full work rights, but age limit 35 and AUD 4,600 fee.
post-study-work.reasoning: The Temporary Graduate (subclass 485) gives up to 2 years for a coursework master's (3 for research) with unrestricted work for both partners, but the general age limit is 35, COVID-era extensions are being scrapped by mid-2026, and the application charge doubled to AUD 4,600 from 1 March 2026.
evidence: 485 up to 2 years for coursework master's, full work rights from day one
General age limit 35 (50 for research master's/PhD)
485 fee AUD 4,600 since 1 Mar 2026; COVID extensions ending

spouse-family.summary: Partner of a master's student gets UNRESTRICTED work hours; full work rights on 485 and skilled visas too.
spouse-family.reasoning: Both partners are software engineers, and a spouse of a student who has commenced a master's or doctorate can work unrestricted hours (not the 48-hour fortnight cap), with full work rights continuing as a dependant on the 485 graduate and skilled visas; only minor friction is the pre-course-start work ban.
evidence: Spouse of master's/doctorate student: unrestricted work hours
Full partner work rights on 485 and skilled/PR visas
Spouse may not work before the student's course starts

links.title: Study Australia student visa work hours

msc-study.summary: Wide English MSc offering and spouse can accompany, but tuition, living costs and visa fees are very high.
msc-study.reasoning: Strong universities offer English-taught master's and students may work up to 48 hours per fortnight, with the spouse able to accompany and work unrestricted, but international tuition is steep, proof of AUD 29,710 funds plus an AUD 2,000 visa charge is required, and managed-growth caps tighten places.
evidence: Broad English-taught master's; work up to 48 hours/fortnight during term
High tuition + AUD 29,710 living-cost proof + AUD 2,000 visa charge
2026 managed-growth student caps (planning level 295,000)

pr-pathway.summary: Skilled PR exists but ICT invitations now need ~90-110 points; employer route via SID->186 is ~2 years.
pr-pathway.reasoning: General Skilled Migration (189/190) has a nominal 65-point pass mark, but 2025-26 ICT invitation rounds effectively required roughly 90-110 points, making independent PR unpredictable; the new Skills in Demand visa offers a clearer employer-sponsored 186 PR after ~2 years but needs a sponsoring employer and shortage-aligned occupation.
evidence: ICT 189/190 invitations effectively need ~90-110 points (2025-26 rounds)
Skills in Demand visa replaced 482 (Dec 2024); 4-year stay, 186 PR after ~2 years
Permanent program 185,000 places, skilled stream ~132,200

links.title: Home Affairs Skills in Demand visa (subclass 482)

income-cost.summary: High tech salaries but Sydney/Melbourne among the world's costliest housing markets.
income-cost.reasoning: Median software-engineer pay of roughly AUD 128k-151k supports good dual-income savings, but housing and living costs in Sydney and Melbourne are among the highest globally, eroding net savings for a couple.
evidence: Median SW engineer pay ~AUD 128k-151k
Sydney/Melbourne housing among the most expensive worldwide

links.title: Australian Bureau of Statistics cost of living

healthcare.summary: Excellent Medicare for PRs/citizens, but Bangladeshis have no reciprocal deal so OSHC/private until PR.
healthcare.reasoning: Medicare gives permanent residents and citizens high-quality, low-cost universal care, but Bangladesh has no Reciprocal Health Care Agreement, so the couple must hold OSHC during study and private cover on the 485 visa until they reach PR.
evidence: Medicare for PRs and citizens (universal, high quality)
No Australia-Bangladesh reciprocal agreement; OSHC/private cover until PR
Adequate health insurance a 485 visa condition

culture-language.summary: English-native and highly multicultural; no language bar for study, work, PR or citizenship.
culture-language.reasoning: English is the everyday and working language, and the large multicultural population with established migrant communities makes integration straightforward; no separate language barrier applies to the migration or citizenship pathway.
evidence: English working and everyday language
Highly multicultural population; no language bar for PR/citizenship

safety-law.summary: Strong rule of law (WJP 11/143, score 0.80) and generally low violent crime.
safety-law.reasoning: Australia ranks 11th of 143 in the 2025 WJP Rule of Law Index (score 0.80) with stable courts, low violent crime and anti-discrimination protections, though it slipped slightly on checks and balances.
evidence: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025: 11th of 143, score 0.80
Generally low violent crime; migrant anti-discrimination protections

politics.summary: Stable democracy, but a sustained 2024-2026 migration crackdown adds real uncertainty.
politics.reasoning: Institutions are stable, yet the Labor government's managed-growth agenda has driven frequent restrictions since 2024 (student caps, Evidence Level 3, doubled 485/student fees, points tightening), creating policy risk for newcomers.
evidence: Stable parliamentary democracy
Managed-growth crackdown: student caps, EL3, fee hikes, 485 cuts (2024-26)
Net overseas migration cut from 429k peak toward ~306k

tax.summary: Progressive income tax to 45% plus 2% Medicare levy; moderate-to-high burden.
tax.reasoning: Resident rates for 2025-26 run 16% to 45% with a 2% Medicare levy (47% top effective rate over AUD 190k), so a tech couple faces a moderate-to-high burden though the tax-free threshold cushions lower income.
evidence: 2025-26 brackets: 16/30/37/45% plus 2% Medicare levy
Top effective rate 47% over AUD 190,000; tax-free under AUD 18,200

links.title: Australian Taxation Office individual tax rates

muslim-diaspora.summary: Large Muslim and Bangladeshi communities, halal everywhere, but rising post-2024 Islamophobia.
muslim-diaspora.reasoning: Australia has ~813,000 Muslims (3.2%) and a sizeable Bangladeshi community (~51,000, mostly in NSW/Victoria) with mosques and halal food ubiquitous in Sydney (Lakemba, Auburn) and Melbourne; however, anti-Muslim incidents have risen since late 2024.
evidence: ~813,000 Muslims (3.2%); ~51,000 Bangladesh-born, mostly NSW/Victoria
Mosques and halal food ubiquitous in Sydney/Melbourne
Rising Islamophobia incidents reported 2024-2025
